etymology.description: semantic 尸(尸) (shī, "body") + phonetic 吊(吊) (diào, "hanging") -> something that hangs from the body
etymology.interpretation: The character 屌(dǐao, "male genitalia") combines the semantic component 尸(尸) (shī, "body") with the phonetic component 吊(吊) (diào, "hanging") to convey the idea of something hanging from the body, referring to male genitalia. The slang sense of 'cocky' links to boldness and assertiveness, metaphorically extending from the physical feature.

examples.chinese: 他总是表现得很屌(他總是表現得很屌)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zǒng shì biǎoxiàn de hěn diǎo.

examples.chinese: 那件事情让他觉得自己很屌(那件事情讓他覺得自己很屌)。
examples.pinyin: Nà jiàn shìqíng ràng tā juéde zìjǐ hěn diǎo.
examples.english: That incident made him feel very cocky.

examples.chinese: 他以为自己很屌，其实并没有(他以為自己很屌，其實並沒有)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yǐwéi zìjǐ hěn diǎo, qíshí bìng méiyǒu.
examples.english: He thinks he is very cocky, but he isn't.
